evt-badminton:
# 🏸 Badminton Rules — Spectrum '26

## 1. Tournament Format

Each team match consists of five games:

1. Men's Singles
2. Women's Singles
3. Men's Doubles
4. Women's Doubles
5. Mixed Doubles

A team wins the match by winning at least **3 out of 5 games**.

Each game follows a **best-of-3 sets** format in the league stage, with each set played to **21 points**. The scoring format for knockout stages may differ and will be communicated after registrations close.

### Team Composition

- Minimum 4 male players
- Minimum 3 female players
- Maximum 12 players (including guest players)

Teams must submit player details in advance. All players will undergo verification prior to participation. Additional players may be added later provided they have not represented or registered for any other team.

### Guest Player Rules

- Maximum 1 male guest player in the playing team
- Maximum 1 female guest player in the playing team
- Once a guest player has played for a team, they cannot represent any other team
- Only one female player is allowed to repeat in a match
- A female guest player cannot repeat and may play only one game per match

### Pool Stage

- Initial rounds will determine the top 16 teams
- These teams will be divided into 4 pools of 4 teams each (round-robin format)
- Pool structure and qualification criteria may be modified at the discretion of the organizers

## 2. General Rules

- **Official shuttle:** Yonex Mavis 350
- All matches conducted in accordance with **BWF rules**; umpire's decision is final
- Inappropriate behavior may result in point deduction or disciplinary action
- **Non-marking badminton shoes** are mandatory — players without appropriate footwear will not be allowed to play
- Failure to comply with any rules may result in disqualification
- Final decision in all matters rests with the Convenor and GSAC members

### Match Scheduling

- Match schedule shared at least one day in advance
- Changes only permitted with mutual agreement between teams and organizer approval
- Teams must report at scheduled time — failure results in disqualification
- If court closing time approaches with pending matches, they will be rescheduled at organizers' discretion

### Team Conduct

- Each team must appoint one team leader as sole point of contact
- Only the team captain may approach organizers in case of dispute
- Misconduct by other players may lead to disqualification or point deduction
- Teams found deliberately underperforming or manipulating outcomes will be disqualified

### Tiebreak (Pool Standings)

1. Number of matches won
2. Game difference
3. Point difference
4. Head-to-head result
evt-basketball:
# 🏀 Basketball Rules — Spectrum '26

## Rules of the Game

### Time-Based

1. The game consists of **4 quarters of 10 minutes** each. Running time for group stage (clock stops only for free throws and fouls), except the final minute of the final quarter which runs on referee time. Finals run on referee time throughout.
2. **Overtime:** 5 minutes if scores tied after regulation. Subsequent overtimes: 2 minutes each.
3. Break between quarters: 2 minutes. Half-time: 5 minutes.
4. **Timeouts:** 1 minute each. One per quarter for first three quarters, 2 in the final quarter. Can only be called when team has possession.
5. **24-second shot clock**, resets to 14 seconds on hitting the offensive rim.
6. Ball must cross half-court within **8 seconds** of live ball.
7. **5 seconds** for throw-in once referee hands ball to player; 5 seconds for free throw.
8. Player has **8 seconds** to start a dribble when holding the ball, and **5 seconds** to release after stopping a dribble.
9. **3-second violation:** No player may stay in the opponent's restricted area for more than 3 consecutive seconds while their team controls a live ball in the front court.
10. Bench will blow whistle at end of quarter time, countdown last 10 seconds, and give heads-up at 1 minute and 30 seconds remaining.
11. If the ball has left contact with a player before time expires and the shot goes in, it counts.

### Fouls

1. All fouls based on **FIBA rules** (offensive charge, reaching, etc.).
2. **4 team fouls** = 2 free throws. Every subsequent foul on a non-shooting player grants 2 free throws. Team foul count resets each quarter. **5 individual fouls** = ejection.
3. All referee calls are final and not subject to change.
4. Teams arguing with a referee may receive a **technical foul**.
5. Bench must maintain distance from sideline as directed by referees — violation results in a technical foul.

## Team Composition

1. Minimum **5 members**, maximum **12 members**.
2. Substitutions allowed during timeouts or intervals — unlimited substitutions from the 12-member roster.
3. If a team cannot field 5 players within **15 minutes** of scheduled time, it results in a forfeit.
4. **2 guest players** allowed per roster for the entire tournament. Guest cannot play for any other team. Maximum **1 guest on court** at any time. Guests may be current institute members or alumni.

## General Conduct

1. Proper sports/basketball shoes and attire required. No full-sleeved shirts — sleeves must end above elbows. Shirts must be tucked in.
2. Toss winner chooses side or bibs; other team takes the remaining choice.
3. **Nails must be clipped.** No jewelry or accessories (watches, bangles, etc.) while playing.
4. Teams unable to make a scheduled match must inform conveners **24 hours** before. Failure to do so results in a 100-point penalty from overall department Spectrum score.

## Tournament Structure

- **9–11 teams:** 3 pools (chit-picking), round-robin within pools. Pool winners → semifinals. 4th semifinalist decided by round-robin among 2nd-place teams. Seeded by point difference: 1v3 and 2v4.
- **12+ teams:** 4 pools with at least 3 teams each, round-robin. Pool winners → semifinals, seeded by point difference: 1v3 and 2v4.
- **Fewer than 9 teams:** 2 pools, round-robin. 1st place teams → semifinals. 2nd of Pool A vs 3rd of Pool B (and vice versa) for remaining semifinal spots. Seeded by point difference: 1v3 and 2v4.

**Point difference:** +PD for winning team, −PD for losing team, accumulated across all matches.

> For any rule not listed here, the **FIBA handbook** will be referred to. Rules are subject to change upon discussion and agreement with all captains.
evt-carrom:
# 🎯 Carrom Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Format

- Team of **5 players** playing 1 singles and 2 doubles
- Each team must have at least one female player
- **Singles:** Player 1
- **Doubles 1:** Player 2 and Player 3
- **Doubles 2:** Player 4 and Player 5
- Team which wins **2 of 3 games** wins the match
- All games will be conducted simultaneously
- The playing order (singles/doubles assignments) must be submitted before every match

## Match Rules

All matches follow the rules at [indiancarrom.co.in](http://www.indiancarrom.co.in/laws-of-carrom/)

### Key Rules

- Each match goes on for **25 points or 45 minutes**, whichever is earlier
- **Queen** = 3 points, other tokens = 1 point each
  - No additional points for queen once you reach 22
- **No communication** during a match (even during doubles) — can be ruled as a foul
- **15 seconds** per shot — failing to shoot in time results in missing your turn
- Players may **not move their chair** while playing (moving within the chair is allowed)
- **Thumb and scissor shots** are allowed
- Playing coins anywhere on the board is allowed
- Break can be made in any way (straight or rebound)

## Penalties / Fouls

| Situation | Penalty |
|-----------|---------|
| Pocket just the striker | 1 token returned, lose turn |
| Pocket striker with a token | 2 tokens returned, turn continues |
| Pocket queen and striker | Queen + 1 of your tokens returned, lose turn |
| Pocket striker while taking cover | 2 tokens returned, turn continues, still get chance to cover queen |
| Pocket your own last coin with striker | Same rule follows (opponent wins if you pocket theirs) |
| Pocket your/opponent's last token with queen still on board | Opponent wins by 3 + number of remaining tokens |
| Coins jumping over the board | Placed at centre, must pay a fine |

> If there is any doubt regarding the rules, it will be cleared before match day.
evt-chess:
# ♟️ Chess Rules — Spectrum '26

## Individual Tournament

### Format

- **Swiss System — Blitz (5+3)**
- Number of rounds determined based on total registrations
- Pairings follow the standard Swiss system

### Time Control

| Stage | Time Control |
|-------|-------------|
| All Rounds | 5 min + 3 sec increment (Blitz) |

### Tiebreak Rules

1. **1st place tie:** Armageddon game
2. **Other places:** Standard tiebreak criteria (Buchholz or similar)
3. Final decision on tiebreak method taken by the arbiters

### General Rules

- FIDE rules and etiquette strictly followed
- Decisions of arbiters are final and binding

---

## Team Tournament

### Format

- **Group Stage + Double Elimination**
- Tournament format and time controls may be adjusted based on logistics

### Team Composition

- **4 open players + 1 female player + 2 reserve players**
- Each department may register up to 2 teams
- No changes to team composition permitted once group matches begin

### Match Structure

- Each match played over **5 boards**, with at least 1 female player participating each round
- 2 substitutes per team, operated under a rolling substitution system

### Board Numbering

| Number | Role |
|--------|------|
| 1 | Main team — Board 1 |
| 2 | Main team — Board 2 |
| 3 | Main team — Board 3 |
| 4 | Main team — Board 4 |
| 5 | Main team — Board 5 (Female) |
| 6 | Substitute 1 |
| 7 | Substitute 2 |

- When a substitute is introduced, they play on Board 4; remaining players shift up accordingly
- **Board 5 (Female Board)** can only be substituted by another female player
- If a female player competes on an open board, she may be substituted on the female board by any female player chosen by the captain

### Guest Player Rules

- Maximum **2 guest players** per roster
- Only **1 guest player** may participate in any single match

### Time Controls

| Stage | Time Control |
|-------|-------------|
| Group Stage | 10 min + 5 sec increment |
| Double Elimination | 30 min + 30 sec increment |

### Tiebreak Rules

**Group Stage:**
1. Sum of game points scored across all matches
2. Armageddon: each team's captain nominates one player for a deciding game

**Double Elimination:**
1. 5 vs 5 Blitz (5+3 time control)
2. If still tied, 1 vs 1 Armageddon game

### Player Changes

- Teams may replace **one player** after Group Stage if there is a confirmed scheduling conflict with another sport
- Teams may modify board order after Group Stage

---

## Contact

- Ayush Priyadarshi — 83038 71628 (ayushp@iisc.ac.in)
- Rajat Narandekar — 88568 98789 (rajatn@iisc.ac.in)

> Rules subject to revision at the organisers' discretion.
evt-cycling:
# 🚴 Cycling Rules — Spectrum '26

## Categories

1. Men Road Bike
2. Women Road Bike
3. Men Hybrid / Mountain Bike
4. Women Hybrid / Mountain Bike

> Road bikes and non-road bikes are distinguished based on **drop handlebars only**. If fewer than 5 road bike participants register per category, all participants race in a single category.

## Event Structure

### Qualifiers (Individual Time Trial)

- Held only if **>20 registrations** per category
- Starting times provided at least a day before
- Race timer starts at mentioned time regardless of participant starting
- **2 laps** of the route
- After 2 laps, head straight to finish at Main Building entrance on Gulmohar Marg
- **Draft-illegal:** minimum **5m separation** between riders (ideally 50m). Overtake quickly or fall back
- Ride must be recorded on **Strava/Wahoo/Komoot/Garmin** to verify no shortcuts
- Re-trial may be allowed if affected by traffic obstruction (at organizers' discretion)

### Finals (Road Race)

| Category | Laps |
|----------|------|
| Men Road Bike | 7 + 1 |
| Women Road Bike | 4 + 1 |
| Men Hybrid / Mountain Bike | 5 + 1 |
| Women Hybrid / Mountain Bike | 2 + 1 |

- **Rolling start** behind a motorized vehicle at 10–15 km/h, pulling away at E-block entrance
- Rider on pole (fastest qualifier) dictates pace after E-block — no one may overtake the pole
- Shuffling among non-pole finalists is permitted
- **Overtakes permitted** after the right turn towards Digits
- Last lap warning announced when starting final lap
- Winner is the rider who crosses the finish line first on Gulmohar Marg
- After the winner crosses, the right turn towards Digits is closed; lapped riders finish at end of current lap
- Race must be GPS-recorded (at minimum 1st, 2nd, 3rd positions)

## Route

![Cycling Route Map](rules/cycling-route.png)

- Starts at Library entrance on Gulmohar Marg
- Loop includes fast straights, tricky turns, and short climbs
- Route marshals at checkpoints to prevent route-cutting

## Safety & Equipment

- **Helmets mandatory** — no helmet, no ride. Ensure proper fit
- **Working brakes** required
- **No e-bikes** (no battery/fuel powered motors)
- Participants should bring water/glucose and essentials

## General Instructions

- Racing numbers distributed before each slot
- All participants must report at reporting time regardless of individual start time
- Safety instructions and any route changes announced at reporting

## Contact

- Rohit — 9442894962 (rohitsuryak@iisc.ac.in)
evt-foosball:
# 🎮 Foosball Rules — Spectrum '26

## Match Structure

Matches are played between departments. Each team plays **3 games** in the following order:

1. Open Doubles
2. Mixed Doubles
3. Open Doubles

## Team Composition

- Only **one player** may participate in a maximum of **2 matches**
- All other players may play only **1 match** each
- Teams may have **1 additional backup player** (apart from the main 5). The backup may only participate if they have not played for any other team

## Gameplay Rules

- Each game is played to **10 points** (5 points from each side of the table)
- Teams **switch sides** after one team reaches 5 points
- **Coin toss** determines the initial serve
- After the serve, the ball must touch at least **2 foosball figures** before a goal can be scored
- A **self-goal** scored in a single touch is valid
- If the ball is **trapped**, it must be released or passed within **15 seconds**
- If the ball becomes **dead at the defenders**, play restarts from defender position once the opposing team is ready
- If the ball goes **out of the table** or becomes unreachable, it will be re-served by the team that last served
- Each team is allowed **1 timeout per match**

## Fair Play and Fouls

The referee's decision is **final** in all situations.

**Not allowed:**
- Distracting the opponent in any form
- Shaking, lifting, or tilting the table
- Spinning the rods beyond 360 degrees

**Penalties for violations:**
1. Warning
2. Loss of point
3. Forfeit of the match

> Tournament format and match schedule will be finalized after registration, based on total number of teams, to ensure fair and structured competition.
evt-frisbee:
# 🥏 Ultimate Frisbee Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Requirements

- **7 players** on the line with a minimum of **3 female players**
- **3 guest players** allowed on the line
- **Gendered pull** will be followed (details explained to captains after registration)
- All players must have **proper sports shoes** for the turf and follow turf guidelines

## Scoring & Match Duration

- Maximum points: **15**
- **Halftime:** 3-minute break at 8 points or at the halfway mark of total game time (whichever is earlier)
- Each team gets **1 timeout of 75 seconds** per game
- All timeouts including halftime are included in game time
- Match time details released based on number of registrations

## Punctuality

- **Game time starts on schedule** regardless of whether a team is on the line
- Teams are expected to arrive **30 minutes** before the game
- For every **5 minutes late**, the opponent is awarded **1 point**
- **20 minutes late** or forfeit = opponent wins **15–0**
- This rule is strictly followed unless reason is specified beforehand or in case of clashes with other sports

## Game Observer

- A game observer will be on the sideline for all matches
- If a conflict arises and the observer's ruling is requested, it is the **final decision** for both teams

## Contact & Spirit

- Frisbee is a **non-contact sport** — captains are responsible for ensuring safe play by team members
- Minor contacts are acceptable, but player safety must be ensured

## Spirit Circle & MVP

- After each game, both teams traditionally sit together in a **Spirit Circle** to discuss the game
- Teams decide whether to follow this tradition, but captains **must submit the MVP** from the opposite team to the convenors after each game

## Venue

IISc Gymkhana Turf
evt-handball:
# 🤾 Handball Rules — Spectrum '26

## 1. Scoring

A goal is scored when the whole of the ball passes between the goalposts and travels fully over the goal line. A goal is not awarded until the referee has signalled and is confident no rules have been broken.

At the end of the match, the team with the most goals wins. If both teams have the same score, officials will decide whether extra time or a penalty shoot-out determines the winner.

## 2. Match Duration

- **League game:** 2 x 10-minute halves, 5-minute break
- **Semi-final:** 2 x 15-minute halves, 5-minute break
- **Final:** 2 x 20-minute halves, 7-minute break

## 3. General Rules

- A team cannot keep possession without attempting to attack
- The start is awarded to the team that wins the coin toss
- A match begins with both teams in their own half
- A goal can be scored from any type of throw

### Ball Handling

- A player can run with the ball for **3 steps maximum**
- A player can hold the ball for up to **3 seconds maximum**
- A player can continuously dribble, providing they bounce the ball
- A player can take 3 steps maximum before and after dribbling (no "double dribble")

### Fouls & Contact

- Players are not able to endanger an opponent with the ball
- Players are not permitted to pull, hit or punch the ball out of an opponent's hands
- Players cannot make contact with the ball below the knee
- Players cannot dive on the floor to regain a loose ball
- A player is allowed to use the torso to obstruct an opponent with or without the ball
- A player cannot outstretch arms or legs to obstruct, push, hold, trip or hit
- An attacking player is not allowed to charge into a defensive player

### Throw-ins & Free Throws

- A throw-in is awarded when the ball goes out of bounds; the thrower must place one foot on the side-line to execute the throw
- **All opposing players must stay 3 m away from the throw-in**
- All minor fouls or violations are penalised with a free-throw taken at the place of infringement

## 4. Officials

A game is officiated by four officials — two referees, a scorekeeper, and a timekeeper. The referees are positioned diagonally on court to observe a side line each. Depending on who is attacking, one is called the field referee and the other the goal referee.

Officials use a whistle and 18 hand signals to communicate decisions, and use a yellow and red card system to warn or remove players.

## 5. Playing Area

A competitive handball court is **40 m long x 20 m wide**, with a goal in the centre of each end.

### Court Markings

- **Centre line** — exactly in the middle of the two goal lines, with a centre spot for starting and restarting play
- **Free-throw line (9 m)** — dashed D-shaped line, 9 m from the outer goal line; used to resume play if an attacker is fouled within the area
- **Penalty mark (7 m)** — located 7 m in front of the goal line, 1 m wide; used for all penalty throws
- **Goal Area / Crease / D-Zone (6 m)** — D-shaped area formed by two connected quarter-circles with 6 m radius; only the goalkeeper is allowed in this area
- **Goalkeeper line (4 m)** — goalkeeper cannot pass this point when defending a penalty shot

## 6. Players

A team consists of **7 players per side** (including goalkeeper). All outfield players are interchangeable during the game.

| Position | Role |
|----------|------|
| **Goalkeeper** | Defends the goal |
| **Left Wing** | Covers left side; provides counter-attacks down the left |
| **Right Wing** | Same as left wing on the opposite side |
| **Left Back** | Stands left of centre back; prevents shooting, initiates counter-attacks |
| **Right Back** | Same as left back on the opposite side |
| **Centre Back** | Middle of court; provides defending and attacking options |
| **Pivot** | Travels along opponent's 6 m line; works with centre back to initiate attacks |

## 7. Guest Rule

Maximum **2 guest players** are allowed on the field as per Spectrum regulation. A team can register any number of guest players but only 2 may be on the field at a time.

## 8. Walkover

If a team gives a walkover for a match, **5 goal points** are awarded to the corresponding opponent team.
evt-hockey:
# 🏑 Hockey Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Formation

- Teams formed from **individual registrations** and may be **mixed across departments**
- Each team: **12–16 players**, with **11 on field** including a goalkeeper

## Match Format

- Played on **full ground** following standard format
- **4 quarters of 15 minutes** each, with short breaks
- **FIH rules** followed for gameplay

## Substitutions

- **Rolling substitutions** (on/off allowed during play)

## Match Day

- Teams must report **10–15 minutes** before match time
- Umpire/organizer decisions are **final**

## Tiebreak

- In case of a draw: **shootout**
- If still tied: **sudden death**

## Safety

- Basic safety gear is recommended
- Unsafe play may lead to **removal from the match**
- Organizers may make minor adjustments based on logistics

## Venue

Gymkhana Hockey Turf, IISc

## Contact

- Siddharth — 9120393393
- Saurav — 9162534885
evt-kabaddi:
# 🤼 Kabaddi Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Composition

- Squad: at most **12 members**, **7 on court** at a time
- Any number of guest players allowed (must be current IISc students)
- A player enrolled for a team **cannot play for other teams**
- Substitution rules as per **AKFI**

## Weight Restrictions

- **Men:** at or below 85 kg
- **Women:** at or below 75 kg

## Match Duration

| Category | Regular | Semis/Finals |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| **Men's** | 30 min (2 × 15 min, 5-min break) | 40 min |
| **Women's** | 20 min (2 × 10 min) | 30 min |

## Scoring

- **1 point** per defender out (awarded to raider/chasing team)
- **Bonus line:** 1 point if raider crosses bonus line with **6+ players** on the ground. No bonus point with 5 or fewer defenders
- **Do-or-die raid** as per AKFI rules

## League Scoring

- **3 points** for a win, **0 points** for a loss
- No draws — tied matches decided by tiebreak procedure

## Tiebreak

1. **5 alternate raids** by different raiders from each team
2. If still tied: **Golden Raid** after a toss
3. No substitution allowed during tiebreak or Golden Raid

## Lobby Rule (PKL Interpretation)

Once the raider goes out of bounds before a tackle, the raid is considered over. Any defender who steps into the lobby in this situation is **not treated as out**.

## General Rules

- All players must **remove jewelry/accessories** and keep **nails trimmed**
- Referee's decision is final

## Contact

- 6303190090
- +91 90844 31077
evt-kho-kho:
# 🤸 Kho-Kho Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Composition

- Squad: at least **9**, at most **12** members
- **9 players** on court at a time

## Match Format

- **2 innings**, each of **14 minutes** (7 min chase + 7 min defend)
- **5-minute break** after each chase/defend period
- **Finals:** 9 minutes per inning

## Scoring

- **1 point** per defender out (awarded to chasing team)
- Total score = number of defenders who got out

## Tournament Format

- Depending on number of teams: **Knockout or League** format
- League: **2 points** for a win, **0 for a loss**

## Tiebreak Rules

### Match Tie

- **Sudden death match** for 1 inning
- The time taken for the chasing team to catch the **first opponent runner** determines the winner (lesser time wins)

### League Tie (same points after all matches)

| Situation | Resolution |
|-----------|-----------|
| Top 2 teams tied | Both qualify for finals |
| 2nd & 3rd or 3rd & 4th tied | Sudden death match |
| Top 3 teams tied | Round-robin sudden death |

## General Rules

- **KKFI rules** apply for any aspects not discussed above
- Referee's decision is **final** — arguments will not be entertained

## Contact

- Suresh — 6381911360
evt-powerlifting:
# 💪 Powerlifting Rules — Spectrum '26

## General Rules

- Competition consists of three lifts in order: **Squat, Bench Press, Deadlift**
- Each lifter gets **3 attempts per lift** — best successful attempt counts
- **Total = best Squat + best Bench Press + best Deadlift**
- Tie in total: **lighter lifter ranks higher**

## Weight Categories

### Men

| Category | Range |
|----------|-------|
| Below 60 kg | up to 60.0 kg |
| 60–70 kg | 60.01 to 70.0 kg |
| 70–80 kg | 70.01 to 80.0 kg |
| 80–90 kg | 80.01 to 90.0 kg |
| Open (90+) | above 90.0 kg |

### Women

| Category | Range |
|----------|-------|
| Below 50 kg | up to 50.0 kg |
| 50–60 kg | 50.01 to 60.0 kg |
| 60–70 kg | 60.01 to 70.0 kg |
| 70–80 kg | 70.01 to 80.0 kg |
| Open (80+) | above 80.0 kg |

## Weigh-In

- Centralized weigh-in before competition
- Each lifter weighed **once** (unless outside chosen weight class)
- Institutional ID card required

## Equipment & Attire

| Equipment | Details |
|-----------|---------|
| **Belts** | Leather or non-stretch, up to 10cm wide, 13mm thick |
| **Wrist wraps** | Allowed |
| **Knee wraps** | Allowed (cannot combine with knee sleeves) |
| **Knee sleeves** | Allowed (cannot combine with wraps) |
| **Chalk** | Provided |
| **Deadlift straps** | Allowed for deadlifts only (bring your own) |
| **Footwear** | Barefoot allowed; clean shoes recommended |

## Rules of Performance

### Squat (Smith Machine)

- Descend until thighs are at least **parallel** (~90° at the knee)
- Controlled descent and ascent required
- No bouncing or rebounding off the bottom
- Knees should not be locked out harshly at the top

### Bench Press

- Head, shoulders, and glutes must stay in contact with the bench
- Feet must remain **flat on the floor**
- Commands: **"Start"** → lower to chest → pause → **"Press"** → lock out → **"Rack"**
- Bar must be lowered to chest, paused motionless, then pressed upward

### Deadlift

- Bar lifted to erect position with **knees locked, shoulders back**
- Hold motionless until referee gives **"Down"** command
- Bar lowered under control with both hands
- Straps allowed, but no hooks that secure to the bar

## Scoring

| Placement | Points |
|-----------|--------|
| 1st (Gold) | 100 |
| 2nd (Silver) | 80 |
| 3rd (Bronze) | 60 |
| 4th | 40 |

## Referees & Safety

- Certified or designated trained officials judge the lifts
- Spotters present for safety during all lifts
- A lift is valid if the referee gives a **green signal**
- Referee and organizing committee have **final authority** on all matters
evt-snooker:
# 🎱 Snooker Rules — Spectrum '26

## Categories

1. Open Singles
2. Men's Doubles
3. Mixed Doubles
4. Women's Doubles

All matches follow a **knockout format**. Guest participation follows general Spectrum rules.

## Men's Doubles Format

| Round | Format |
|-------|--------|
| **Rounds 1 & 2** | 1 singles (30 min, senior table) + 1 singles (30 min, junior table) + 1 doubles tiebreaker (45 min) |
| **Quarterfinals** | 2 singles (30 min each, both tables) + 1 doubles tiebreaker (45 min) |
| **Semifinals** | 3 singles on each table + 1 doubles tiebreaker |
| **Finals** | 3 singles on each table + 3 doubles |

## Mixed Doubles Format

| Round | Format |
|-------|--------|
| **Rounds 1 & 2** | Same as Men's Doubles |
| **Quarterfinals** | 1 singles (45 min, each table) + 1 doubles tiebreaker (60 min) |
| **Semifinals** | 2 singles (45 min each, each table) + 1 doubles tiebreaker (60 min) |
| **Finals** | 2 singles on each table + 3 doubles (60 min each) |

## Women's Doubles

Format depends on number of registrations.

## General Rules

- Players must **pre-decide table allocation** and cannot switch during the match
- In cases of **Free Ball, Touching Ball, or disputes**, the referee's decision is final
- For complete rules, refer to the official [WPBSA rulebook](https://wpbsa.com/wp-content/uploads/2198_WPBSA-Rulebook-2024-25.pdf)
evt-table-tennis:
# 🏓 Table Tennis Rules — Spectrum '26

## Tournament Structure

### 1. Knockout Rounds

- Two rounds of knockout to determine the **top 8 teams**
- Match format: **Best of 3 sets**

### 2. League Stage

- 8 qualified teams divided into **2 pools of 4 teams** each
- **Round-robin** format within each pool
- Match format: **Best of 5 sets**

### 3. Semifinals & Finals

- **Top 2 teams from each pool** advance to semifinals
- Semifinal and final: **Best of 5 sets**

## Seeding

Top 8 teams from **Spectrum 2025** receive seeding.

## Match Format

Each match consists of up to **5 games** in this order:

1. Men's Singles
2. Women's Singles
3. Open Singles
4. Open Singles
5. Open Doubles

**First team to win 3 games wins the match.**

## Player Rules

- Each team consists of **5 players**
- A player can participate in at most **1 singles + 1 doubles** match per team match
- Only **1 guest player** allowed per team
- In case of injury, if not all 5 players have been used throughout the tournament, a **replacement** may be found
- **Umpire's decision is final**
evt-throwball:
# 🏐 Throwball Rules — Spectrum '26

## Team Composition

- **12 players** per team: 7 active + 5 substitutes
- Maximum **2 guest players** per team — teams with more than 2 guests will be disqualified

## Court & Net

- Court: **12.2m × 18.3m** with 1m neutral box on either side
- Net height: **2.2m**

## Match Format

- **Best of 3 sets**, 25 points per set (rally scoring)
- Toss winner chooses service or court
- Team that served in 1st set receives in 2nd set

## Player Positions

- Players must stand in a **2-3-2 position** at the time of service
- At least **3 players** must serve per team, alternating each time after a side-over

## Service Rules

- Ball must be released from **above shoulder level**
- Served using the **palm** — ball should not touch any other body part
- Must serve **within 5 seconds** after the whistle
- Service ball must **not touch the net**
- Server must not enter the court before the ball crosses the net
- Serve from the **service zone** without crossing the end line

## Rally Rules

- Players must **catch with both hands** and **return with one hand** only
- Ball must be released **within 3 seconds** of being caught
- **Double touches** not allowed (service or rally)
- Ball falling on the **box line or dead zone** = foul
- If ball slips or rebounds from one player's hands, another player **cannot catch and return** it (foul)
- No body part should touch the box line while receiving or returning
- Players may **shift the ball** from right to left or vice versa
- A player can **jump when throwing** (ball may touch the net but not the antenna); must have ground contact when catching
- **Simultaneous catching** by 2+ players = foul (clashing)

## Timeouts

- **2 timeouts of 30 seconds** per set

## Disputes

- Only the team **captain** may speak to the referee
- Referee's decision is **final** — arguments lead to team disqualification
- In case of crowd/disturbance interference, referee may call a **re-serve**

## Contact

- Srinithya — 82771 82290
- Baradh — 89257 71396
evt-volleyball:
# 🏐 Volleyball Rules — Spectrum '26

## Eligibility

Open tournament for all **IISc community members** with valid ID: students, staff, professors, post-docs, research associates, project associates, alumni, interns, security, mess workers, Samadhan staff, and anyone with a valid IISc or Gymkhana ID card.

Family members of staff/faculty not directly related to IISc are **not eligible** (unless they hold a valid Gymkhana ID).

## Team Composition

- Up to **12 players** per team
- Each player enrolled in **one team only** — no external participants
- Maximum **2 teams per department**
- Player details submitted via Google form; verification before each game

## Guest Player Rules

- Any eligible person playing for a department other than their own is a **guest player**
- **Alumni** are guests in all departments
- Maximum **2 guest players on the ground** at any time
- Once a guest plays for a department, they **cannot play for any other** team
- Even playing for **1 point** locks the guest to that team
- Guests can be substituted with other guests without breaking the 2-on-field limit

## Tournament Structure

- Teams divided into **pools** → league matches (+ knockouts if needed) → quarterfinals (if needed) → semifinals → 3rd-place match → final
- After registration, teams may withdraw before knockout/league matches

## Match Format

- League/knockout: **Best of 3 sets** (25-25-15 points)
- Semis/finals: **Best of 3 or 5 sets** (by agreement between teams)

## Game Rules

- **FIVB rules** with the exception of **no rotation** (fixed player positions)

## Scoring (League)

| Result | Points |
|--------|--------|
| Win | 2 |
| Technical forfeit | 1 |
| Loss | 0 |

## Tiebreak

In case of tied league scores: **sets won** → **point difference** → team with the most sets won or highest point difference advances.

## Schedule

- Matches scheduled **5 PM – 12 AM**
- Holiday morning hours may also be used if needed
- Organizing committee reserves the right to change schedule

## Match Day Rules

- **10-minute** grace period before walkover
- Team refusing to play without valid reason = **eliminated** (previous results voided)
- **No on-spot registrations**

## Code of Conduct

- No abuse or quarreling with opponents or referee
- Warnings given first to player and captain; continued violations → **team disqualified** from Spectrum
- Referee's decision is **final** — serious disputes may result in disqualification

## Complaints

- Must be raised with referee or organizing committee **before or during** the match
- Post-match complaints **will not be entertained**
- Any team found cheating or violating rules → **immediate disqualification**

## Contact

- 7842815191
- 7466993970
